page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed, backed by a 320 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rates placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark memory scores are strong, ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls into the average category. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds reach 63.39 GB/sec and 58.36 GB/sec respectively. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model is strong, though performance drops to the bottom 25% when executing text generation on a medium 7B model.

Given its high local NVMe SSD capacity and DDR5 memory density relative to vCPUs, this instance offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads without requiring a massive vCPU footprint. This makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications that require substantial local storage and memory but do not scale linearly with high core counts. The hardware configuration is suited for database operations, transactional workloads, caching layers, and data-intensive applications requiring fast local storage access.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores in the top 10%, alongside strong memory performance. Multi-core benchmarks and database operations sit in the average tier. With an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and high-capacity local NVMe storage, this instance provides a balanced profile for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for database hosting, caching systems, and data-intensive applications requiring rapid local storage access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5000 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for data-intensive workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized server combining high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage with DDR5 memory and strong single-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 8 vCPUs and a 3.6 GHz clock speed
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded compression speeds
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-intensive applications
